To Staff counsel:
I am the City Attorney for the City of Benicia, and am writing on
behalf of a nenber of the Benicia City Council to request written
advice with respect to the Council nenberr s duties under the Fair
Political 
Practi-ces Act. 
The Council member has discussed the
issue with ne and authorized this request. This request is nade
pursuant to covernnent Code Section 83114(b). The specific issues
and material facts, as they have been related to me, are described

SKY VALLEY BENICIA
The City of Benicia is currently processi.ng a Specific Plan for an
approxinately 5,000 acres area of land located in Solano County,
and commonLy referred to as rrSky Valley.tr The plan area is alnost
cornpJ.etely undeveloped. The land is currently used primarily for
cattl,e grazing and passive recreation activities. 
The plan area is
for the most part located outside Benicia's current city limits.
County zoning is exclusive agricultural requiring a rnininum of 20
acres per parcel for developnent purposes.
The Specific Plan being considered by the city proposes the
development of a high quality residential cornrnunity consisting of
5,360 homes, Ancillary uses proposed for the area include parks,
schools, a neighborhood shopping center, and large areas of open
space. The proposed plan also shot s an 18-ho1e golf course to be
open for public use. A copy of a diagran of the proposed Land use
plan is included for your reference.
The primary freelray access to the planned area is

COIJNCILMAN FI'LTON I S PROPERTY INTERESTS
councj.lman Dirk Fulton and his wife own fee title to an undeveloped
1.82 acre parcel bounded by East Second Street, Lake Herman Road,
and Interstate 680. The property is located within the Benicia
city lirnits, and is zoned commercj-aI general. This zoning allows
a wide variety of cornnercia1 uses, The property is encumbered by
a cal Trans right-of-way along its Lake Herman Road frontage. 
A
Cal Trans encroachment perrnit would be required in order to gain
access to the parcel from Lake Herman Road. such permits are
difficult 
to obtain and access to the parcel would 1ike1y be
obtained via Second street.
The parcel is located more than 4,500 ft. 
from the nearest
boundaries of the sky vaIley Plan area. The property is well over
one nile from the closest area to be developed within the plan
area. There are well over ten parcels under separate ownership
within a 2,5oo ft. radius of the Fulton parcel.
FUTURE CITY COIJNCIL ACTIONS REOUIRED
In order to adopt and inpleurent the Specific Pfan, the city counci]
will have to take several different actions. Fi.rst, the Council
rnust certify an environmental inpact report completed pursuant to
the requirements of the California Environnental Quality Act which
identifies and discusses the environnental impact of the proposed
p1an. Then, it will be necessary for the City Council to adopt a
General Plan Amendnent, since the cityrs current General Plan does
not envision the type and scope of development currently proposed
in the Sky Valley Specific e1an, Additionally, it is anticipated
that the City will enter into a development agreenent with some or
all- of the developers and property owners in the plan area. It
will 
be necessary for the Council to reviev and approve the
development agreenent.
If approved and implenented, the sky VaIIey Specific Plan will have
a profound effect on the city of Benicia. It would add several- new
parks to the cityrs park systen, rniles of new roads, sewers, and
storn drains, and would increase the cityrs current population by
at least 40 percent. Councilnan Fulton would most certainly like
to participate, to the greatest extent possible, in decisions that
will implenent this p1an, and brinq such trenendous change to the
lives of his constituents. However, councilnan Fulton is cognizant
of the requirernents of the Fair Political Practices Act, and does
not wish €o breach his obligations under the 1aw. Accordingly, I
request, on behalf of councilnan Fulton, that the Comnission review
the facts as explained in this request, and provide councilnan
Fulton with written advice as to vrhether he nay participate in any
or all of the planning actions the city Council- will take on the

sections.question: Whether Councilman Dirk Fulton may participate in any or all of the planning actions the City Council will take on the proposed Sky Valley Specific Plan, given his property interests in the area.
sections.question_synthetic: Can Councilman Fulton, who owns a 1.82-acre parcel located over one mile from the Sky Valley Plan area, participate in City Council decisions regarding the adoption and implementation of the Specific Plan without violating the Fair Political Practices Act?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or short answer section present. The request seeks advice on potential conflicts of interest under the Fair Political Practices Act. Document is from City Attorney (requester) to FPPC, dated June 8, 1992.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Councilman Dirk Fulton may participate in any or all of the planning actions the City Council will take on the proposed Sky Valley Specific Plan, given his property interests in the area.

embedding.summary: City Attorney for Benicia requests written advice from FPPC on behalf of Councilman Dirk Fulton regarding whether he may participate in City Council actions on the Sky Valley Specific Plan, which involves developing 5,000 acres into a residential community, given that Fulton owns property that could potentially be affected by the plan's infrastructure improvements.
